summary: Split a large portion of the massive game-mode translation unit src/melee/gm/gm_1601.c into several smaller units: gmresult.c, gmresultplayer.c, gm_17AD.c, gm_17BA.c, gmregcommon.c, and gm_17C0.c, with matching updates to GALE01 splits, symbols, build configuration, and headers. The PR explicitly deferred a full header split because many files still include gm_1601.h; instead gm_1601.h was kept as a compatibility surface and made to include the new gm_17C0.h.
what_changed: Reduced gm_1601.c by deleting about 904 lines from the address range beginning around fn_80174274 through the later gm_8019B860 area.

Added new source units for contiguous address ranges: gmresult.c at 0x80174274-0x80177724, gmresultplayer.c at 0x80177724-0x8017AD04, gm_17AD.c at 0x8017AD04-0x8017BACC, gm_17BA.c at 0x8017BACC-0x8017BE84, gmregcommon.c at 0x8017BE84-0x8017C0C8, and gm_17C0.c at 0x8017C0C8-0x8019B8C4.
Updated config/GALE01/splits.txt so text, rodata, data, bss, sdata, sbss, and sdata2 ranges previously attributed to gm_1601.c are partitioned across the new object files.
Updated configure.py to add the new gm objects as NonMatching build objects immediately after gm_1601.c.
Moved many prototypes from gm_1601.h into the new gm_17C0.h and made gm_1601.h include <melee/gm/gm_17C0.h> to preserve existing include behavior.
Kept most new files including gm_1601.h and gm_1601.static.h rather than fully untangling shared declarations and statics.
Adjusted several data-string sizes in config/GALE01/symbols.txt around lbl_803D6898, lbl_803D6934, lbl_803D6940, and lbl_804D3F70 through lbl_804D3FBC.

The decomp.dev bot report showed 22 matches moved out of gm_1601 into the new units, 22 corresponding broken matches in gm_1601, one unmatched-function improvement for fn_801904D0 in gm_17C0, and many apparent regressions that reflect the unit move rather than necessarily lost code.
smart_moves: Used exact split boundaries from the original address order instead of trying to reorganize by speculative semantics only, which keeps the linker map and matching work tractable.

Introduced semantic filenames where there was evidence or convention, such as gmresult.c, gmresultplayer.c, and gmregcommon.c, while retaining address-based names like gm_17AD.c, gm_17BA.c, and gm_17C0.c where better names were not established.
Avoided a risky all-at-once include graph rewrite by keeping gm_1601.h as a compatibility umbrella and only extracting gm_17C0.h for the large trailing section.
Updated splits.txt, symbols.txt, and configure.py in the same PR as the source movement, preventing the source split from becoming disconnected from build and matching metadata.
Preserved existing nonmatching placeholders with /// #function comments in the new files, so future work can continue at the correct addresses without losing the known function list.

Kept the moved simple matching functions in the appropriate new units, such as fn_80174274 in gmresult.c, fn_80179F6C in gmresultplayer.c, gm_8017BE84 in gmregcommon.c, and several small accessors/helpers in gm_17C0.c.
decomp_lessons: For huge legacy translation units, a mechanical split by contiguous address ranges can be valuable even before all headers and types are cleanly separated.

When many downstream files include an old monolithic header, an umbrella-header transition can reduce churn: add the new header under the old include rather than forcing every dependent file to change immediately.
A TU split should update configure.py and GALE01 splits at the same time; otherwise decomp.dev and map comparisons will report misleading regressions or missing objects.
Bot match reports after a file split should be interpreted as movement between units: new matches in the new unit and broken matches in the old unit may be the expected result of relocating code.
Symbol string-size corrections can be necessary when data ranges are repartitioned, because the new object boundaries expose or rely on more precise data annotations.
Leaving address-based file names for unclear regions is preferable to inventing semantic names without evidence.
naming_conventions: Known or inferred game-mode result regions use semantic names: gmresult.c and gmresultplayer.c.
Common register/helper region was named gmregcommon.c, matching the PR title's gmregcommon terminology.
Unidentified contiguous regions retained the address-derived gm_17AD.c, gm_17BA.c, and gm_17C0.c convention.
Function names remained existing address-based names such as fn_80174274, gm_8017BE84, and gm_80188454; this PR did not try to rename them semantically.
New header gm_17C0.h follows the address-derived source filename rather than creating a broad semantic header name.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Split the former gm_1601 object at exact text boundaries: 0x80174274, 0x80177724, 0x8017AD04, 0x8017BACC, 0x8017BE84, and 0x8017C0C8.
Carried over nonmatching placeholders using /// #symbol comments to preserve function order and decomp.me-style tracking.
Kept small already-matching C functions in the new destination objects so they continue to produce the same code at their new unit locations.
Adjusted rodata/data/bss/sdata/sbss/sdata2 ranges in splits.txt for every new object, not just .text.
Added all new objects as NonMatching in configure.py, matching the status of the original gm_1601.c object.
Used gm_1601.static.h in the new files so moved functions could still access existing static labels and structs while the split was being staged.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body notes a deliberate limitation: most headers were not split because many files include gm_1601.h.
The decomp.dev bot reported 22 new matches in the new units and 22 corresponding broken matches in gm_1601, consistent with relocation of matched functions across translation units.
The bot also reported one unmatched-function improvement for gm_17C0/fn_801904D0 from 0.00% to 80.00%, while listing many regressions under gm_1601 caused by the moved address range.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that split gm_1601.h or removed the compatibility include of gm_17C0.h.
Search for subsequent semantic renames of gm_17AD.c, gm_17BA.c, or gm_17C0.c.
Check whether gmresult.c and gmresultplayer.c later gained dedicated headers instead of relying on gm_1601.h.
Look for follow-up fixes to symbols.txt string sizes near lbl_803D6898 and lbl_804D3F70 after this split.
Review later matching work on fn_801904D0 in gm_17C0, which was reported as 80% after this PR.
